2605.14889.title: SurgicalMamba: Dual-Path SSD with State Regramming for Online Surgical Phase Recognition
2605.14889.paper_summary: Online surgical phase recognition (SPR) underpins context-aware operating-room systems and requires committing to a prediction at every frame from past context alone. Surgical video poses three demands that natural-video recognizers do not jointly address: procedures span tens of thousands of frames, time flows non-uniformly as long routine stretches are punctuated by brief phase-defining transitions, and the visual domain is narrow so backbone features are strongly correlated across channels. Existing recognizers either let per-frame cost grow with elapsed length, or hold cost bounded but advance state at a uniform rate with channel-independent dynamics, leaving the latter two demands unaddressed. We present SurgicalMamba, a causal SPR model built on Mamba2's structured state-space duality (SSD) that holds per-frame cost at O(d). It introduces three SSD-compatible components, each targeting one demand: a dual-path SSD block that separates long- and short-term regimes at the level of recurrent state; intensity-modulated stepping, a continuous-time time-warp that adapts the slow path's effective rate to phase-relevant information; and state regramming, a per-chunk Cayley rotation that opens cross-channel mixing in the otherwise axis-aligned SSM recurrence. The learned rotation planes inherit a phase-aligned structure without any direct supervision, offering an interpretable internal signature of surgical workflow. Across seven public SPR benchmarks, SurgicalMamba reaches state-of-the-art accuracy and phase-level Jaccard under strict online evaluation: 94.6%/82.7% on Cholec80 (+0.7 pp/+2.2 pp over the strongest prior) and 89.5%/68.9% on AutoLaparo (+1.7 pp/+2.0 pp), at 119 fps on a single GPU. Ablations isolate the contribution of each component. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/sukjuoh/Surgical-Mamba.
2605.14889.paper_summary_zh: 在線手術階段識別（SPR）支撐著具上下文感知的手術室系統，並需要僅根據過去的上下文在每一幀上做出預測。手術視頻提出了三個自然視頻識別器無法共同解決的需求：手術過程跨越數萬幀，時間流動不均勻，長時間的例行操作被短暫的階段定義轉換所打斷，並且視覺領域狹窄，因此主幹特徵在通道之間高度相關。現有的識別器要麼讓每幀的成本隨著經過的長度增長，要麼保持成本有界但以通道無關的動態以均勻的速度推進狀態，從而未能解決後兩個需求。我們提出了SurgicalMamba，一個基於Mamba2的結構化狀態空間對偶性（SSD）的因果SPR模型，它將每幀的成本保持在O(d)。它引入了三個兼容SSD的組件，每個組件針對一個需求：一個雙通道SSD模塊，在重複狀態層面上分離長期和短期範疇；強度調制步進，一種連續時間的時間扭曲，將慢通道的有效速率調整為與階段相關的信息；以及狀態重編程，一種每塊的Cayley旋轉，打開了在其他軸對齊的SSM重複中進行跨通道混合。學習到的旋轉平面在沒有任何直接監督的情況下繼承了階段對齊的結構，提供了手術工作流程的可解釋內部特徵。在七個公共SPR基準測試中，SurgicalMamba在嚴格的在線評估下達到最先進的準確性和階段級Jaccard：在Cholec80上達到94.6%/82.7%（比最強的先前提高0.7 pp/+2.2 pp），在AutoLaparo上達到89.5%/68.9%（比最強的先前提高1.7 pp/+2.0 pp），在單個GPU上以119 fps運行。消融實驗隔離了每個組件的貢獻。代碼可在https://github.com/sukjuoh/Surgical-Mamba上公開獲得。
2605.14889.author: Sukju Oh et.al.
